title: The Foxglove King
author: Hannah Whitten
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Foxglove King" book by Hannah Whitten?
Options:
A. Lore glowered at Remaut's retreating form, holding up her skirt to avoid the water. Remaut is just as caught up in everything as she is. But Gabriel is loyal only to Apollius. As fun as Lore may be, Remaut doesn't think she can compete with Apollius. If Remaut gets an opportunity to take advantage of Lore for Apollius, he will. Remaut turned to look at her, the firelight giving his dark hair a sinister glow while shadowing his eyes. It seems I'm the only one in the Citadel who knows who Lore is and what she's capable of, and isn't trying to manipulate her.
B. Lore scowled at his back, gathering her hem high to avoid the water. Hes just as caught up in all this as I am. Be that as it may, Gabriels loyalty is to one person alone. And diverting as you are, Lore, I dont think you can compete with Apollius. If the opportunity arises for Remaut to use you in service to his god, hell take it. He turned to face her, the flame gilding his dark hair in fiendish light, keeping his eyes in shadow. In fact, it seems like Im the only person in the Citadel who knows who you are and what youre capable of, and isnt trying to make you a tool.
C. Lore frowned at Remaut's back as he walked away, lifting her dress to stay dry. Remaut is as involved in this situation as she is. However, Gabriel's allegiance is only to Apollius. Although Lore is entertaining, she can't compare to Apollius in Remaut's eyes. If Remaut gets a chance to exploit Lore for Apollius's benefit, he will seize it. Remaut turned to face her, the firelight making his dark hair look evil but keeping his eyes obscured. Indeed, it seems like I'm the only one in the Citadel who knows Lore's identity and abilities, and isn't trying to use her. 
D. Lore glared at Remaut's back as he walked away, lifting her dress hem clear of the water. Remaut is as involved in this as she is. However, Gabriel is devoted solely to Apollius. Entertaining as Lore may be, Remaut doesn't believe she can measure up to Apollius. If Remaut gets a chance to use Lore for Apollius's benefit, he'll take it. Remaut turned to face her, the firelight making his dark hair look evil while keeping his eyes obscured. It seems I'm the only one in the Citadel who understands Lore's identity and abilities, and isn't attempting to exploit her.
